#!/bin/bash

# Make this file executable
chmod +x "$0" 2>/dev/null || true

# =============================================================================
# SuperMac - Main Dispatcher
# =============================================================================
# Professional command-line tool for macOS with organized, powerful shortcuts
# 
# Usage: mac <category> <action> [arguments]
# 
# Built by CosmoLabs - https://cosmolabs.org
# Repository: https://github.com/CosmoLabs-org/SuperMac
# License: MIT
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail  # Strict error handling

# =============================================================================
# Bootstrap and Load Utilities
# =============================================================================

# Get the directory where this script is located
readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
readonly SUPERMAC_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
readonly LIB_DIR="$SUPERMAC_ROOT/lib"

# Load shared utilities first
if [[ -f "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh" ]]; then
    # shellcheck source=../lib/utils.sh
    source "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh"
else
    echo "ERROR: SuperMac utilities not found at $LIB_DIR/utils.sh" >&2
    echo "Please ensure SuperMac is properly installed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# =============================================================================
# Configuration & Constants
# =============================================================================

readonly SUPERMAC_VERSION="2.1.0"
readonly MIN_MACOS_VERSION="10.15"

# Define available categories and their metadata
declare -A CATEGORIES=(
    ["finder"]="📁:File visibility and Finder management"
    ["wifi"]="🌐:WiFi control and management"
    ["network"]="📡:Network information and troubleshooting"
    ["system"]="🖥️:System information and maintenance"
    ["dev"]="💻:Developer tools and utilities"
    ["display"]="🖥️:Display and appearance settings"
    ["dock"]="🚢:Dock management and positioning"
    ["audio"]="🔊:Audio control and device management"
    ["screenshot"]="📸:Screenshot settings and management"
)

# Global shortcuts (bypass category structure)
declare -A GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS=(
    ["ip"]="network:ip"
    ["cleanup"]="system:cleanup"
    ["restart-finder"]="finder:restart"
    ["kp"]="dev:kill-port"
    ["vol"]="audio:volume"
    ["dark"]="display:dark-mode"
    ["light"]="display:light-mode"
    ["help"]="show_help"
    ["version"]="show_version"
)

# =============================================================================
# Help System
# =============================================================================

show_main_help() {
    print_supermac_header
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Usage:${NC} mac <category> <action> [arguments]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Professional command-line shortcuts for macOS developers and power users${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    print_category_header "Available Categories" "📋" 65
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
        printf "${PURPLE}│${NC}  %-12s %s %-40s ${PURPLE}│${NC}\n" "$category" "$emoji" "$description"
    done
    
    print_category_footer 65
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Quick Commands:"
    echo "  mac help <category>         Show commands for specific category"
    echo "  mac search <term>           Search commands by keyword"
    echo "  mac version                 Show version information"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Examples:"
    echo "  mac finder restart          # Restart Finder"
    echo "  mac wifi toggle             # Toggle WiFi on/off"
    echo "  mac system info             # Show system information"
    echo "  mac dev kill-port 3000      # Kill process on port 3000"
    echo "  mac display dark-mode       # Switch to dark mode"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Global Shortcuts:"
    echo "  mac ip                      # Quick IP address lookup"
    echo "  mac cleanup                 # Quick system cleanup"
    echo "  mac dark / mac light        # Quick appearance toggle"
    echo ""
    
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built with ❤️ by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR${NC}"
    echo ""
}

show_category_help() {
    local category="$1"
    
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module to get its help
    load_module "$category"
    
    # Call the category's help function
    if declare -f "${category}_help" >/dev/null; then
        "${category}_help"
    else
        print_error "Help not available for category: $category"
        print_info "The module may not be fully implemented yet."
    fi
}

show_version() {
    echo -e "${BOLD}$SUPERMAC_NAME v$SUPERMAC_VERSION${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR - $SUPERMAC_URL${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "System Information:"
    echo "  macOS: $(sw_vers -productName) $(get_macos_version)"
    echo "  Build: $(get_macos_build)"
    echo "  Architecture: $(get_architecture)"
    echo "  Shell: $(get_shell)"
    echo "  SuperMac Location: $SUPERMAC_ROOT"
    echo ""
    echo "Available Categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}License: MIT${NC}"
}

search_commands() {
    local search_term="$1"
    search_term=$(to_lower "$search_term")
    
    print_header "🔍 Search Results for: $search_term"
    echo ""
    
    local found=false
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        # Load module to search its commands
        if load_module "$category" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Get commands from module (this would need to be implemented in each module)
            if declare -f "${category}_search" >/dev/null; then
                local results
                results=$("${category}_search" "$search_term")
                if [[ -n "$results" ]]; then
                    IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
                    print_category_header "$category" "$emoji" 50
                    echo "$results"
                    print_category_footer 50
                    echo ""
                    found=true
                fi
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "$found" != true ]]; then
        print_warning "No commands found matching '$search_term'"
        echo ""
        print_info "Try broader search terms or use 'mac help' to see all available commands"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Command Validation & Routing
# =============================================================================

validate_environment() {
    # Check macOS version
    if ! check_macos; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if modules directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$LIB_DIR" ]]; then
        die "SuperMac library directory not found: $LIB_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided
    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Call the category's dispatcher function
    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Handle no arguments
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
        show_main_help
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Parse arguments
    local category="$1"
    local action="${2:-}"
    
    # Handle special commands
    case "$category" in
        "help"|"-h"|"--help")
            if [[ -n "$action" ]]; then
                show_category_help "$action"
            else
                show_main_help
            fi
            return 0
            ;;
        "version"|"-v"|"--version")
            show_version
            return 0
            ;;
        "search")
            if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
                print_error "Search term required"
                print_info "Usage: mac search <term>"
                return 1
            fi
            search_commands "$action"
            return 0
            ;;
        "debug")
            enable_debug
            print_success "Debug mode enabled"
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
        "--debug")
            enable_debug
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    route_command "$@"
}

# =============================================================================
# Script Execution
# =============================================================================

# Only run main if script is executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided
    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Call the category's dispatcher function
    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Handle no arguments
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
        show_main_help
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Parse arguments
    local category="$1"
    local action="${2:-}"
    
    # Handle special commands
    case "$category" in
        "help"|"-h"|"--help")
            if [[ -n "$action" ]]; then
                show_category_help "$action"
            else
                show_main_help
            fi
            return 0
            ;;
        "version"|"-v"|"--version")
            show_version
            return 0
            ;;
        "search")
            if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
                print_error "Search term required"
                print_info "Usage: mac search <term>"
                return 1
            fi
            search_commands "$action"
            return 0
            ;;
        "debug")
            enable_debug
            print_success "Debug mode enabled"
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
        "--debug")
            enable_debug
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    route_command "$@"
}

# =============================================================================
# Script Execution
# =============================================================================

# Only run main if script is executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

content.text:
This is a pre-existing issue — `declare -A` is Bash 4+ only and macOS ships 3.2. This isn't something my changes introduced. The audit didn't flag it either since it was masked by the root `mac` file being used differently. This needs a separate fix (use `declare -A` with `#!/usr/bin/env bash` pointing to a Bash 4+, or refactor to use parallel arrays).

Not in scope for Phase 0. Let me commit what we've done and note this as a new bug.

content.text:
# Commit-All v2 — CCS-Delegated Intelligent Commit Workflow

CCS handles mechanical scanning and grouping. Claude handles creative message writing and review decisions.

## Usage

```bash
/commit-all                    # Analyze and commit all changes
/commit-all --session          # Only changes from this session
/commit-all master --push      # Commit, merge to master, push
/commit-all --skip-review      # Skip code review for trivial changes
/commit-all --skip-quality     # Skip build/lint/test checks
```

## Execution

### Phase 0: WORKCHECK (optional, 2 seconds)

If an active continuation prompt exists, run `ccs workcheck` to surface any unverified goals or documentation gaps before committing. Display the output to the user but do NOT block on it — proceed to Phase 1 regardless.

Skip if: `--skip-quality` flag is set, or no active prompt exists (`ccs workcheck` returns error).

### Phase 1: ANALYZE (CCS — fast, zero tokens)

Run `ccs commit-analyze --json` (or `--json --session` if `--session` flag).

This returns structured JSON with:
- File changes grouped by semantic proximity
- Quality gate results (build/lint/test)
- Diff summaries per group (regex-extracted function/type/test names)
- Issue references (auto-detected from `docs/issues/` affected_files)
- `review_recommended: true` if total diff > 100 lines

If `--skip-quality` was passed, add `--skip-quality` flag.

### Phase 2: REVIEW + REGROUP (Claude — creative)

Review CCS's suggested groups. For each group, decide:
- Accept the grouping, or reorganize (split/merge) based on session context
- Split groups that mix `feat` and `fix` changes
- Merge groups that are logically the same feature

If `review_recommended` is true and `--skip-review` not set: invoke `superpowers:requesting-code-review` for architectural/security review.

If 3+ groups, create a TaskCreate per group for progress visibility.

### Phase 3: CRAFT MESSAGES (Claude — the main value)

**Principle: Commit messages are permanent history. You're not losing history — you're building it.**

Write commit messages for each group following conventional commit standards. Scale detail to significance:

| Type | Subject | Body | Example Body |
|------|---------|------|-------------|
| **feat** | What it enables | Structured bullets: what each component adds, design decisions, test counts | `- Add TokenBucket with per-key limits and automatic cleanup` / `- Design: sliding window over fixed counters for accuracy vs memory` / `- 21 tests covering burst, expiry, and concurrent access` |
| **fix** | What was broken | Root cause + how it's fixed | `Root cause: race condition in session cleanup — goroutine reads map while reaper deletes.` / `Fix: copy keys slice before iteration, delete under write lock.` |
| **test** | What's covered | Edge cases, why these tests matter | `- 14 tests covering: valid input, boundary values, nil handling, concurrent writes` / `- Regression test for BUG-045 (duplicate session IDs)` |
| **refactor** | What improved | Before/after reasoning | `Before: 340-line monolith handling parse + validate + emit.` / `After: three focused functions, each independently testable.` |
| **perf** | What's optimized + metric | Before/after numbers | `Reduce CLAUDE.md from 461 to 350 lines (~24% reduction, ~660 tokens saved)` |
| **security** | What's hardened | Vulnerability + fix | `Root cause: unsanitized input interpolated into sh -c. Fix: validate via allowlist before interpolation.` |
| **docs** | What's documented | Concise, 1-line body max | `Update API reference for v2 auth endpoints.` |
| **ci** | What pipeline changed | Brief explanation | `Add ast-grep scan step to pre-commit checks.` |
| **build** | What build changed | Brief explanation | `Update Makefile port assignments for monorepo services.` |
| **chore** | What changed | No body needed for metadata | *(none)* |

#### Format Rules

**Subject**: `type(scope): imperative summary` — max 72 chars, lowercase after colon.
**Body**: Blank line after subject. Imperative bullet points: "Add X", "Fix Y", "Remove Z" — never paragraphs.
**Footer**: `Refs: FEAT-xxx` when CCS detected issue refs. `BREAKING CHANGE:` when applicable.

#### Mandatory Rules

- **Never** produce a single-line message for `feat` commits touching 3+ files — the body is required.
- **Always** include the WHY for features: what does this enable? What problem does it solve?
- **Always** include test counts when tests are added: "21 tests covering..." not just "add tests".
- **Always** reference issues/roadmap items from CCS analysis in footer: `Refs: FEAT-xxx, ROAD-yyy`.
- **No AI attribution** — no co-authored-by, no emoji signatures, no "Generated with" lines.
- **No prose paragraphs** — bullets only. Each bullet starts with an imperative verb.

#### Examples

**Bad — too thin** (no body for a multi-file feature):
```
feat(crypto): add crypto module
```

**Bad — over-engineered** (prose wall, repeats the diff):
```
feat(crypto): add crypto module

This commit introduces a comprehensive cryptographic module that provides
encryption and decryption capabilities. The module was designed with careful
consideration of security best practices and performance requirements. We
evaluated several approaches including AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305, and
XSalsa20 before settling on AES-256-GCM as the primary cipher suite due
to hardware acceleration support on modern processors...
(200 more words)
```

**Good — structured, scaled to significance**:
```
feat(crypto): add AES-256-GCM encryption module

- Add Encrypt/Decrypt with authenticated data support
- Add key derivation from passphrase via Argon2id
- Design: AES-GCM over ChaCha20 for hardware acceleration on target infra
- 18 tests covering: roundtrip, bad key, tampered ciphertext, empty input

Refs: FEAT-091
```

**Good — fix with root cause**:
```
fix(reaper): prevent panic on concurrent session cleanup

Root cause: goroutine iterates session map while reaper deletes entries.
Fix: snapshot keys under read lock, delete individually under write lock.

Refs: BUG-072
```

**Good — chore, no body needed**:
```
chore: update Go dependencies to latest patch versions
```

### Integration Check (Advisory)

If an `integration-manifest.yaml` exists in the project root or `docs/`, and the staged changes span 2+ platform directories (check with `git diff --cached --name-only` against the manifest's platform paths):

```
⚠️ Changes span {platform1} + {platform2} — consider: ccs audit integration --quick
```

This is advisory only — display the suggestion, do not auto-run. Skip if no manifest exists.

### Phase 4: EXECUTE (CCS — fast)

Build JSON plan and pipe to `ccs commit-batch`:
```json
[
  {"files": ["path/file1.go", "path/file2.go"], "message": "feat(scope): subject\n\nBody"},
  {"files": ["path/file3_test.go"], "message": "test(scope): subject"}
]
```

Run: `echo '<plan>' | ccs commit-batch --json`

Or with `--dry-run` first to preview.

### Phase 5: POST (optional)

- If target branch specified: `ccs merge <target>`
- If `--push`: `ccs sync --push`

## Output

```
✅ Created N semantic commits
   1. abc1234 feat(auth): implement JWT authentication
   2. def5678 docs(auth): document auth endpoints
   3. ghi9012 test(auth): add auth coverage

[If merged:] ✅ Merged to: master
[If pushed:] ✅ Pushed to remote
```

## Note on Versioning

Version bumps are NOT automatic. Use `/release` for proper releases.

## Related

`/release` • `/pit-stop` • `/merge` • `ccs commit-analyze` • `ccs commit-batch`

**Phase 2: Review + Regroup**

CCS suggested 6 groups. I'll reorganize into 4 logical commits:

1. **Bug fixes** (lib/ changes) — BUG-001, 002, 003, 005, 006 + version fix
2. **Install fix + dispatcher** — BUG-004 + bin/mac version dedup
3. **Delete root duplicates + hygiene** — BUG-008 + .gitignore + LICENSE + CLAUDE.md
4. **Docs + metadata** — roadmap, transcripts, GOrchestra intel

No code review needed — these are all targeted single-line/small fixes from audit.

**Phase 3: Craft messages**

content.text:
# /please-continue - Resume Interrupted Workflow

Signal that you're done with your interruption and Claude should resume exactly where it left off.

## Usage

```bash
/please-continue
```

No arguments. No flags. Just resume.

## Instructions

When invoked:

1. **Check for unmerged agent work** — run `git worktree list` and check for agent worktrees from this session that haven't been merged. If found, merge them first via `ccs merge`.
2. **Check uncommitted work** — `git status --short` for anything that needs committing
3. **Check TaskList** — if tasks exist, find the current in_progress task
4. **Review conversation history** — identify what you were doing before the interruption
5. **Acknowledge the interruption** — briefly confirm any new context the user provided
6. **Resume the workflow** — pick up at the exact step you were on

### Rules

- Do NOT restart the task from scratch
- Do NOT re-read files already in context
- Do NOT re-run commands already executed
- If the user's interruption changes the plan, adapt — don't ignore it
- Always check for background agents that may have completed while the user was talking

### Output

```
Resuming: [brief description of what you were doing]
[If unmerged agents]: Merging N agent worktrees first...
[If user added context]: Noted: [1-line acknowledgment]
```

Then continue working silently — no ceremony needed.

## Related

`/remind-me` * `/whatsup` * `/recall`

input.command: ccs issues create bug "bin/mac uses declare -A which requires Bash 4+ (macOS ships 3.2)" --description "The main dispatcher (bin/mac) uses 'declare -A CATEGORIES' for associative arrays at line 47. This requires Bash 4+ but macOS ships Bash 3.2 as /bin/bash. Running 'bash bin/mac help' produces: 'line 47: finder: unbound variable'. The script shebang is #!/bin/bash which always uses the macOS default 3.2. Fix options: (1) Refactor to parallel arrays compatible with Bash 3.2, (2) Change shebang to #!/usr/bin/env bash and document Bash 4+ requirement, (3) Add runtime Bash version check with clear error message." --severity high --fix-direction "Replace declare -A with Bash 3.2-compatible data structure (parallel arrays or case statements), or add Bash version guard at script start" --affected-files "bin/mac" -v

content.text:
# /workcheck — Mid-Session Goal Verification

Answers: **"Did I finish what I started?"** Goes back to the original plan and verifies each goal against TaskList + git evidence.

## Usage

```bash
/workcheck              # Full verification against active prompt
```

## Execution

### Step 1: Gather Data

Run in parallel:
1. `ccs workcheck --json` via Bash
2. TaskList

### Step 2: Read Plan Sources

From the JSON, read `prompt_path`, `brainstorm_ref`, `plan_ref`. Skip missing refs.

### Step 3: Verify Each Goal

Match each goal against TaskList subjects, commit messages, and files changed.
Classify: DONE | PARTIAL | NOT STARTED | BLOCKED

### Step 4: Detect Drift

Commits matching no goal = NEW (unplanned work).

### Step 5: Auto-File Discoveries

File genuine bugs/ideas found during verification.

### Step 6: Output

Structured table with per-goal status, drift section, doc gaps, and recommendation.

## Related

`/remind-me` | `/sweep` | `/what-now` · `/triage` (recommended: lifecycle-aware task selection)

<command-message>please-continue</command-message>
<command-name>/please-continue</command-name>

content.text:
# /please-continue - Resume Interrupted Workflow

Signal that you're done with your interruption and Claude should resume exactly where it left off.

## Usage

```bash
/please-continue
```

No arguments. No flags. Just resume.

## Instructions

When invoked:

1. **Check for unmerged agent work** — run `git worktree list` and check for agent worktrees from this session that haven't been merged. If found, merge them first via `ccs merge`.
2. **Check uncommitted work** — `git status --short` for anything that needs committing
3. **Check TaskList** — if tasks exist, find the current in_progress task
4. **Review conversation history** — identify what you were doing before the interruption
5. **Acknowledge the interruption** — briefly confirm any new context the user provided
6. **Resume the workflow** — pick up at the exact step you were on

### Rules

- Do NOT restart the task from scratch
- Do NOT re-read files already in context
- Do NOT re-run commands already executed
- If the user's interruption changes the plan, adapt — don't ignore it
- Always check for background agents that may have completed while the user was talking

### Output

```
Resuming: [brief description of what you were doing]
[If unmerged agents]: Merging N agent worktrees first...
[If user added context]: Noted: [1-line acknowledgment]
```

Then continue working silently — no ceremony needed.

## Related

`/remind-me` * `/whatsup` * `/recall`

# Configuration & Constants
# =============================================================================

# SUPERMAC_VERSION is defined in lib/utils.sh (single source of truth)
readonly MIN_MACOS_VERSION="10.15"

# Define available categories and their metadata
declare -A CATEGORIES=(
    ["finder"]="📁:File visibility and Finder management"
    ["wifi"]="🌐:WiFi control and management"
    ["network"]="📡:Network information and troubleshooting"
    ["system"]="🖥️:System information and maintenance"
    ["dev"]="💻:Developer tools and utilities"
    ["display"]="🖥️:Display and appearance settings"
    ["dock"]="🚢:Dock management and positioning"
    ["audio"]="🔊:Audio control and device management"
    ["screenshot"]="📸:Screenshot settings and management"
)

# Global shortcuts (bypass category structure)
declare -A GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS=(
    ["ip"]="network:ip"
    ["cleanup"]="system:cleanup"
    ["restart-finder"]="finder:restart"
    ["kp"]="dev:kill-port"
    ["vol"]="audio:volume"
    ["dark"]="display:dark-mode"
    ["light"]="display:light-mode"
    ["help"]="show_help"
    ["version"]="show_version"
)

# =============================================================================
# Help System
# =============================================================================

show_main_help() {
    print_supermac_header
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Usage:${NC} mac <category> <action> [arguments]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Professional command-line shortcuts for macOS developers and power users${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    print_category_header "Available Categories" "📋" 65
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
        printf "${PURPLE}│${NC}  %-12s %s %-40s ${PURPLE}│${NC}\n" "$category" "$emoji" "$description"
    done
    
    print_category_footer 65
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Quick Commands:"
    echo "  mac help <category>         Show commands for specific category"
    echo "  mac search <term>           Search commands by keyword"
    echo "  mac version                 Show version information"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Examples:"
    echo "  mac finder restart          # Restart Finder"
    echo "  mac wifi toggle             # Toggle WiFi on/off"
    echo "  mac system info             # Show system information"
    echo "  mac dev kill-port 3000      # Kill process on port 3000"
    echo "  mac display dark-mode       # Switch to dark mode"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Global Shortcuts:"
    echo "  mac ip                      # Quick IP address lookup"
    echo "  mac cleanup                 # Quick system cleanup"
    echo "  mac dark / mac light        # Quick appearance toggle"
    echo ""
    
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built with ❤️ by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR${NC}"
    echo ""
}

file.content:
show_category_help() {
    local category="$1"
    
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module to get its help
    load_module "$category"
    
    # Call the category's help function
    if declare -f "${category}_help" >/dev/null; then
        "${category}_help"
    else
        print_error "Help not available for category: $category"
        print_info "The module may not be fully implemented yet."
    fi
}

show_version() {
    echo -e "${BOLD}$SUPERMAC_NAME v$SUPERMAC_VERSION${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR - $SUPERMAC_URL${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "System Information:"
    echo "  macOS: $(sw_vers -productName) $(get_macos_version)"
    echo "  Build: $(get_macos_build)"
    echo "  Architecture: $(get_architecture)"
    echo "  Shell: $(get_shell)"
    echo "  SuperMac Location: $SUPERMAC_ROOT"
    echo ""
    echo "Available Categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}License: MIT${NC}"
}

search_commands() {
    local search_term="$1"
    search_term=$(to_lower "$search_term")
    
    print_header "🔍 Search Results for: $search_term"
    echo ""
    
    local found=false
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        # Load module to search its commands
        if load_module "$category" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Get commands from module (this would need to be implemented in each module)
            if declare -f "${category}_search" >/dev/null; then
                local results
                results=$("${category}_search" "$search_term")
                if [[ -n "$results" ]]; then
                    IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
                    print_category_header "$category" "$emoji" 50
                    echo "$results"
                    print_category_footer 50
                    echo ""
                    found=true
                fi
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "$found" != true ]]; then
        print_warning "No commands found matching '$search_term'"
        echo ""
        print_info "Try broader search terms or use 'mac help' to see all available commands"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Command Validation & Routing
# =============================================================================

validate_environment() {
    # Check macOS version
    if ! check_macos; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if modules directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$LIB_DIR" ]]; then
        die "SuperMac library directory not found: $LIB_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

218	route_command() {
219	    local category="$1"
220	    local action="$2"
221	    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
222	    
223	    # Check if it's a global shortcut
224	    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
225	        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
226	        
227	        # Handle special cases
228	        case "$shortcut" in
229	            "show_help")
230	                show_main_help
231	                return 0
232	                ;;
233	            "show_version")
234	                show_version
235	                return 0
236	                ;;
237	            *)
238	                # Parse category:action format
239	                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
240	                category="$real_category"
241	                action="$real_action"
242	                ;;
243	        esac
244	    fi
245	    
246	    # Validate category exists
247	    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
248	        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
249	        echo ""
250	        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
251	        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
252	        return 1
253	    fi
254	    
255	    # Load the category module
256	    if ! load_module "$category"; then
257	        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
258	        return 1
259	    fi
260	    
261	    # Check if action is provided
262	    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
263	        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
264	        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
265	        return 1
266	    fi
267	    
268	    # Call the category's dispatcher function
269	    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
270	    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
271	        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
272	        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
273	    else
274	        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
275	        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
276	        return 1
277	    fi
278	}
279	
280	# =============================================================================
281	# Main Entry Point
282	# =============================================================================
283	
284	main() {
285	    # Validate environment first
286	    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
287	        return 1
288	    fi

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided
    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Call the category's dispatcher function
    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then

            ;;

            return 0

#!/bin/bash

# Make this file executable
chmod +x "$0" 2>/dev/null || true

# =============================================================================
# SuperMac - Main Dispatcher
# =============================================================================
# Professional command-line tool for macOS with organized, powerful shortcuts
# 
# Usage: mac <category> <action> [arguments]
# 
# Built by CosmoLabs - https://cosmolabs.org
# Repository: https://github.com/CosmoLabs-org/SuperMac
# License: MIT
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail  # Strict error handling

# =============================================================================
# Bootstrap and Load Utilities
# =============================================================================

# Get the directory where this script is located
readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
readonly SUPERMAC_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
readonly LIB_DIR="$SUPERMAC_ROOT/lib"

# Load shared utilities first
if [[ -f "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh" ]]; then
    # shellcheck source=../lib/utils.sh
    source "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh"
else
    echo "ERROR: SuperMac utilities not found at $LIB_DIR/utils.sh" >&2
    echo "Please ensure SuperMac is properly installed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# =============================================================================
# Configuration & Constants
# =============================================================================

# SUPERMAC_VERSION is defined in lib/utils.sh (single source of truth)
readonly MIN_MACOS_VERSION="10.15"

# Define available categories and their metadata
declare -A CATEGORIES=(
    ["finder"]="📁:File visibility and Finder management"
    ["wifi"]="🌐:WiFi control and management"
    ["network"]="📡:Network information and troubleshooting"
    ["system"]="🖥️:System information and maintenance"
    ["dev"]="💻:Developer tools and utilities"
    ["display"]="🖥️:Display and appearance settings"
    ["dock"]="🚢:Dock management and positioning"
    ["audio"]="🔊:Audio control and device management"
    ["screenshot"]="📸:Screenshot settings and management"
)

# Global shortcuts (bypass category structure)
declare -A GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS=(
    ["ip"]="network:ip"
    ["cleanup"]="system:cleanup"
    ["restart-finder"]="finder:restart"
    ["kp"]="dev:kill-port"
    ["vol"]="audio:volume"
    ["dark"]="display:dark-mode"
    ["light"]="display:light-mode"
    ["help"]="show_help"
    ["version"]="show_version"
)

# =============================================================================
# Help System
# =============================================================================

show_main_help() {
    print_supermac_header
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Usage:${NC} mac <category> <action> [arguments]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Professional command-line shortcuts for macOS developers and power users${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    print_category_header "Available Categories" "📋" 65
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
        printf "${PURPLE}│${NC}  %-12s %s %-40s ${PURPLE}│${NC}\n" "$category" "$emoji" "$description"
    done
    
    print_category_footer 65
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Quick Commands:"
    echo "  mac help <category>         Show commands for specific category"
    echo "  mac search <term>           Search commands by keyword"
    echo "  mac version                 Show version information"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Examples:"
    echo "  mac finder restart          # Restart Finder"
    echo "  mac wifi toggle             # Toggle WiFi on/off"
    echo "  mac system info             # Show system information"
    echo "  mac dev kill-port 3000      # Kill process on port 3000"
    echo "  mac display dark-mode       # Switch to dark mode"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Global Shortcuts:"
    echo "  mac ip                      # Quick IP address lookup"
    echo "  mac cleanup                 # Quick system cleanup"
    echo "  mac dark / mac light        # Quick appearance toggle"
    echo ""
    
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built with ❤️ by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR${NC}"
    echo ""
}

show_category_help() {
    local category="$1"
    
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module to get its help
    load_module "$category"
    
    # Call the category's help function
    if declare -f "${category}_help" >/dev/null; then
        "${category}_help"
    else
        print_error "Help not available for category: $category"
        print_info "The module may not be fully implemented yet."
    fi
}

show_version() {
    echo -e "${BOLD}$SUPERMAC_NAME v$SUPERMAC_VERSION${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR - $SUPERMAC_URL${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "System Information:"
    echo "  macOS: $(sw_vers -productName) $(get_macos_version)"
    echo "  Build: $(get_macos_build)"
    echo "  Architecture: $(get_architecture)"
    echo "  Shell: $(get_shell)"
    echo "  SuperMac Location: $SUPERMAC_ROOT"
    echo ""
    echo "Available Categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}License: MIT${NC}"
}

search_commands() {
    local search_term="$1"
    search_term=$(to_lower "$search_term")
    
    print_header "🔍 Search Results for: $search_term"
    echo ""
    
    local found=false
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        # Load module to search its commands
        if load_module "$category" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Get commands from module (this would need to be implemented in each module)
            if declare -f "${category}_search" >/dev/null; then
                local results
                results=$("${category}_search" "$search_term")
                if [[ -n "$results" ]]; then
                    IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
                    print_category_header "$category" "$emoji" 50
                    echo "$results"
                    print_category_footer 50
                    echo ""
                    found=true
                fi
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "$found" != true ]]; then
        print_warning "No commands found matching '$search_term'"
        echo ""
        print_info "Try broader search terms or use 'mac help' to see all available commands"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Command Validation & Routing
# =============================================================================

validate_environment() {
    # Check macOS version
    if ! check_macos; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if modules directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$LIB_DIR" ]]; then
        die "SuperMac library directory not found: $LIB_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided
    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Call the category's dispatcher function
    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Handle no arguments
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
        show_main_help
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Parse arguments
    local category="$1"
    local action="${2:-}"
    
    # Handle special commands
    case "$category" in
        "help"|"-h"|"--help")
            if [[ -n "$action" ]]; then
                show_category_help "$action"
            else
                show_main_help
            fi
            return 0
            ;;
        "version"|"-v"|"--version")
            show_version
            return 0
            ;;
        "search")
            if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
                print_error "Search term required"
                print_info "Usage: mac search <term>"
                return 1
            fi
            search_commands "$action"
            return 0
            ;;
        "debug")
            enable_debug
            print_success "Debug mode enabled"
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
        "--debug")
            enable_debug
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    route_command "$@"
}

# =============================================================================
# Script Execution
# =============================================================================

# Only run main if script is executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

    echo ""
    
    local found=false
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        # Load module to search its commands
        if load_module "$category" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Get commands from module (this would need to be implemented in each module)
            if declare -f "${category}_search" >/dev/null; then
                local results
                results=$("${category}_search" "$search_term")
                if [[ -n "$results" ]]; then
                    IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
                    print_category_header "$category" "$emoji" 50
                    echo "$results"
                    print_category_footer 50
                    echo ""
                    found=true
                fi
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "$found" != true ]]; then
        print_warning "No commands found matching '$search_term'"
        echo ""
        print_info "Try broader search terms or use 'mac help' to see all available commands"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Command Validation & Routing
# =============================================================================

validate_environment() {
    # Check macOS version
    if ! check_macos; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if modules directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$LIB_DIR" ]]; then
        die "SuperMac library directory not found: $LIB_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided

        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1

        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Handle no arguments
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
        show_main_help
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Parse arguments
    local category="$1"
    local action="${2:-}"
    
    # Handle special commands
    case "$category" in
        "help"|"-h"|"--help")
            if [[ -n "$action" ]]; then
                show_category_help "$action"
            else
                show_main_help
            fi
            return 0
            ;;
        "version"|"-v"|"--version")
            show_version
            return 0
            ;;
        "search")
            if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
                print_error "Search term required"
                print_info "Usage: mac search <term>"
                return 1
            fi
            search_commands "$action"
            return 0

            enable_debug

            return $?
            ;;
        "--debug")
            enable_debug
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    route_command "$@"

#!/bin/bash

# Make this file executable
chmod +x "$0" 2>/dev/null || true

# =============================================================================
# SuperMac - Main Dispatcher
# =============================================================================
# Professional command-line tool for macOS with organized, powerful shortcuts
# 
# Usage: mac <category> <action> [arguments]
# 
# Built by CosmoLabs - https://cosmolabs.org
# Repository: https://github.com/CosmoLabs-org/SuperMac
# License: MIT
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail  # Strict error handling

# =============================================================================
# Bootstrap and Load Utilities
# =============================================================================

# Get the directory where this script is located
readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
readonly SUPERMAC_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
readonly LIB_DIR="$SUPERMAC_ROOT/lib"

# Load shared utilities first
if [[ -f "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh" ]]; then
    # shellcheck source=../lib/utils.sh
    source "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh"
else
    echo "ERROR: SuperMac utilities not found at $LIB_DIR/utils.sh" >&2
    echo "Please ensure SuperMac is properly installed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# =============================================================================
# Configuration & Constants
# =============================================================================

# SUPERMAC_VERSION is defined in lib/utils.sh (single source of truth)
readonly MIN_MACOS_VERSION="10.15"

# Define available categories (Bash 3.2-compatible: parallel arrays)
readonly CAT_NAMES=(audio dev display dock finder network screenshot system wifi)
readonly CAT_META=(
    "audio:🔊:Audio control and device management"
    "dev:💻:Developer tools and utilities"
    "display:🖥️:Display and appearance settings"
    "dock:🚢:Dock management and positioning"
    "finder:📁:File visibility and Finder management"
    "network:📡:Network information and troubleshooting"
    "screenshot:📸:Screenshot settings and management"
    "system:🖥️:System information and maintenance"
    "wifi:🌐:WiFi control and management"
)

# Look up category metadata by name (returns "emoji:description")
get_category_meta() {
    local name="$1"
    local entry
    for entry in "${CAT_META[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$entry" == "$name:"* ]]; then
            echo "${entry#*:}"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# Check if a category exists
has_category() {
    local name="$1"
    local n
    for n in "${CAT_NAMES[@]}"; do
        [[ "$n" == "$name" ]] && return 0
    done
    return 1
}

# Get sorted category list
get_categories() {
    echo "${CAT_NAMES[@]}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sort
}

# Global shortcuts (Bash 3.2-compatible: case statement)
get_shortcut() {
    case "$1" in
        ip)             echo "network:ip" ;;
        cleanup)        echo "system:cleanup" ;;
        restart-finder) echo "finder:restart" ;;
        kp)             echo "dev:kill-port" ;;
        vol)            echo "audio:volume" ;;
        dark)           echo "display:dark-mode" ;;
        light)          echo "display:light-mode" ;;
        help)           echo "show_help" ;;
        version)        echo "show_version" ;;
        *)              return 1 ;;
    esac
}

# =============================================================================
# Help System
# =============================================================================

show_main_help() {
    print_supermac_header
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Usage:${NC} mac <category> <action> [arguments]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Professional command-line shortcuts for macOS developers and power users${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    print_category_header "Available Categories" "📋" 65
    
    for category in $(get_categories); do
        local meta
        meta=$(get_category_meta "$category")
        IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "$meta"
        printf "${PURPLE}│${NC}  %-12s %s %-40s ${PURPLE}│${NC}\n" "$category" "$emoji" "$description"
    done
    
    print_category_footer 65
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Quick Commands:"
    echo "  mac help <category>         Show commands for specific category"
    echo "  mac search <term>           Search commands by keyword"
    echo "  mac version                 Show version information"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Examples:"
    echo "  mac finder restart          # Restart Finder"
    echo "  mac wifi toggle             # Toggle WiFi on/off"
    echo "  mac system info             # Show system information"
    echo "  mac dev kill-port 3000      # Kill process on port 3000"
    echo "  mac display dark-mode       # Switch to dark mode"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Global Shortcuts:"
    echo "  mac ip                      # Quick IP address lookup"
    echo "  mac cleanup                 # Quick system cleanup"
    echo "  mac dark / mac light        # Quick appearance toggle"
    echo ""
    
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built with ❤️ by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR${NC}"
    echo ""
}

    if [[ ! -v "CATEGORIES[$category]" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(printf '%s ' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories"
        return 1

    # Call the category's help function
    if declare -f "${category}_help" >/dev/null; then
        "${category}_help"
    else

    fi
}

show_version() {

    echo "  SuperMac Location: $SUPERMAC_ROOT"

                    print_category_footer 50
                    echo ""
                    found=true
                fi
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "$found" != true ]]; then
        print_warning "No commands found matching '$search_term'"
        echo ""
        print_info "Try broader search terms or use 'mac help' to see all available commands"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Command Validation & Routing
# =============================================================================

validate_environment() {
    # Check macOS version
    if ! check_macos; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if modules directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$LIB_DIR" ]]; then
        die "SuperMac library directory not found: $LIB_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if ! has_category "$category"; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(get_categories | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided
    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Call the category's dispatcher function
    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Handle no arguments
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
        show_main_help
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Parse arguments
    local category="$1"
    local action="${2:-}"
    
    # Handle special commands
    case "$category" in
        "help"|"-h"|"--help")
            if [[ -n "$action" ]]; then
                show_category_help "$action"
            else
                show_main_help
            fi
            return 0
            ;;
        "version"|"-v"|"--version")
            show_version
            return 0
            ;;
        "search")
            if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
                print_error "Search term required"
                print_info "Usage: mac search <term>"
                return 1
            fi
            search_commands "$action"
            return 0
            ;;
        "debug")
            enable_debug
            print_success "Debug mode enabled"
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
        "--debug")
            enable_debug
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    route_command "$@"
}

# =============================================================================
# Script Execution
# =============================================================================

# Only run main if script is executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi

#!/bin/bash

# Make this file executable
chmod +x "$0" 2>/dev/null || true

# =============================================================================
# SuperMac - Main Dispatcher
# =============================================================================
# Professional command-line tool for macOS with organized, powerful shortcuts
# 
# Usage: mac <category> <action> [arguments]
# 
# Built by CosmoLabs - https://cosmolabs.org
# Repository: https://github.com/CosmoLabs-org/SuperMac
# License: MIT
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail  # Strict error handling

# =============================================================================
# Bootstrap and Load Utilities
# =============================================================================

# Get the directory where this script is located
readonly SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
readonly SUPERMAC_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
readonly LIB_DIR="$SUPERMAC_ROOT/lib"

# Load shared utilities first
if [[ -f "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh" ]]; then
    # shellcheck source=../lib/utils.sh
    source "$LIB_DIR/utils.sh"
else
    echo "ERROR: SuperMac utilities not found at $LIB_DIR/utils.sh" >&2
    echo "Please ensure SuperMac is properly installed." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# =============================================================================
# Configuration & Constants
# =============================================================================

# SUPERMAC_VERSION is defined in lib/utils.sh (single source of truth)
readonly MIN_MACOS_VERSION="10.15"

# Define available categories (Bash 3.2-compatible: parallel arrays)
readonly CAT_NAMES=(audio dev display dock finder network screenshot system wifi)
readonly CAT_META=(
    "audio:🔊:Audio control and device management"
    "dev:💻:Developer tools and utilities"
    "display:🖥️:Display and appearance settings"
    "dock:🚢:Dock management and positioning"
    "finder:📁:File visibility and Finder management"
    "network:📡:Network information and troubleshooting"
    "screenshot:📸:Screenshot settings and management"
    "system:🖥️:System information and maintenance"
    "wifi:🌐:WiFi control and management"
)

# Look up category metadata by name (returns "emoji:description")
get_category_meta() {
    local name="$1"
    local entry
    for entry in "${CAT_META[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$entry" == "$name:"* ]]; then
            echo "${entry#*:}"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# Check if a category exists
has_category() {
    local name="$1"
    local n
    for n in "${CAT_NAMES[@]}"; do
        [[ "$n" == "$name" ]] && return 0
    done
    return 1
}

# Get sorted category list
get_categories() {
    echo "${CAT_NAMES[@]}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sort
}

# Global shortcuts (Bash 3.2-compatible: case statement)
get_shortcut() {
    case "$1" in
        ip)             echo "network:ip" ;;
        cleanup)        echo "system:cleanup" ;;
        restart-finder) echo "finder:restart" ;;
        kp)             echo "dev:kill-port" ;;
        vol)            echo "audio:volume" ;;
        dark)           echo "display:dark-mode" ;;
        light)          echo "display:light-mode" ;;
        help)           echo "show_help" ;;
        version)        echo "show_version" ;;
        *)              return 1 ;;
    esac
}

# =============================================================================
# Help System
# =============================================================================

show_main_help() {
    print_supermac_header
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Usage:${NC} mac <category> <action> [arguments]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Professional command-line shortcuts for macOS developers and power users${NC}"
    echo ""
    
    print_category_header "Available Categories" "📋" 65
    
    for category in $(get_categories); do
        local meta
        meta=$(get_category_meta "$category")
        IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "$meta"
        printf "${PURPLE}│${NC}  %-12s %s %-40s ${PURPLE}│${NC}\n" "$category" "$emoji" "$description"
    done
    
    print_category_footer 65
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Quick Commands:"
    echo "  mac help <category>         Show commands for specific category"
    echo "  mac search <term>           Search commands by keyword"
    echo "  mac version                 Show version information"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Examples:"
    echo "  mac finder restart          # Restart Finder"
    echo "  mac wifi toggle             # Toggle WiFi on/off"
    echo "  mac system info             # Show system information"
    echo "  mac dev kill-port 3000      # Kill process on port 3000"
    echo "  mac display dark-mode       # Switch to dark mode"
    echo ""
    
    print_header "Global Shortcuts:"
    echo "  mac ip                      # Quick IP address lookup"
    echo "  mac cleanup                 # Quick system cleanup"
    echo "  mac dark / mac light        # Quick appearance toggle"
    echo ""
    
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built with ❤️ by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR${NC}"
    echo ""
}

show_category_help() {
    local category="$1"
    
    if ! has_category "$category"; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(get_categories | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module to get its help
    load_module "$category"
    
    # Call the category's help function
    if declare -f "${category}_help" >/dev/null; then
        "${category}_help"
    else
        print_error "Help not available for category: $category"
        print_info "The module may not be fully implemented yet."
    fi
}

show_version() {
    echo -e "${BOLD}$SUPERMAC_NAME v$SUPERMAC_VERSION${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}Built by $SUPERMAC_AUTHOR - $SUPERMAC_URL${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "System Information:"
    echo "  macOS: $(sw_vers -productName) $(get_macos_version)"
    echo "  Build: $(get_macos_build)"
    echo "  Architecture: $(get_architecture)"
    echo "  Shell: $(get_shell)"
    echo "  SuperMac Location: $SUPERMAC_ROOT"
    echo ""
    echo "Available Categories: $(get_categories | tr '\n' ' ')"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${DIM}Repository: $SUPERMAC_REPO${NC}"
    echo -e "${DIM}License: MIT${NC}"
}

search_commands() {
    local search_term="$1"
    search_term=$(to_lower "$search_term")
    
    print_header "🔍 Search Results for: $search_term"
    echo ""
    
    local found=false
    
    for category in $(printf '%s\n' "${!CATEGORIES[@]}" | sort); do
        # Load module to search its commands
        if load_module "$category" 2>/dev/null; then
            # Get commands from module (this would need to be implemented in each module)
            if declare -f "${category}_search" >/dev/null; then
                local results
                results=$("${category}_search" "$search_term")
                if [[ -n "$results" ]]; then
                    IFS=':' read -r emoji description <<< "${CATEGORIES[$category]}"
                    print_category_header "$category" "$emoji" 50
                    echo "$results"
                    print_category_footer 50
                    echo ""
                    found=true
                fi
            fi
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "$found" != true ]]; then
        print_warning "No commands found matching '$search_term'"
        echo ""
        print_info "Try broader search terms or use 'mac help' to see all available commands"
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Command Validation & Routing
# =============================================================================

validate_environment() {
    # Check macOS version
    if ! check_macos; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if modules directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$LIB_DIR" ]]; then
        die "SuperMac library directory not found: $LIB_DIR"
    fi
    
    # Enable debug if requested
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--debug" ]]; then
        enable_debug
        shift
    fi
    
    return 0
}

route_command() {
    local category="$1"
    local action="$2"
    shift 2  # Remove category and action from arguments
    
    # Check if it's a global shortcut
    if [[ -v "GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]" ]]; then
        local shortcut="${GLOBAL_SHORTCUTS[$category]}"
        
        # Handle special cases
        case "$shortcut" in
            "show_help")
                show_main_help
                return 0
                ;;
            "show_version")
                show_version
                return 0
                ;;
            *)
                # Parse category:action format
                IFS=':' read -r real_category real_action <<< "$shortcut"
                category="$real_category"
                action="$real_action"
                ;;
        esac
    fi
    
    # Validate category exists
    if ! has_category "$category"; then
        print_error "Unknown category: $category"
        echo ""
        print_info "Available categories: $(get_categories | tr '\n' ' ')"
        print_info "Use 'mac help' to see all categories and commands"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Load the category module
    if ! load_module "$category"; then
        print_error "Failed to load module: $category"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check if action is provided
    if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
        print_error "Action required for category '$category'"
        print_info "Use 'mac help $category' to see available actions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Call the category's dispatcher function
    local dispatcher_func="${category}_dispatch"
    if declare -f "$dispatcher_func" >/dev/null; then
        print_debug "Dispatching to $category:$action with args: $*"
        "$dispatcher_func" "$action" "$@"
    else
        print_error "Category '$category' is not properly implemented"
        print_info "Missing dispatcher function: $dispatcher_func"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Main Entry Point
# =============================================================================

main() {
    # Validate environment first
    if ! validate_environment "$@"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Handle no arguments
    if [[ $# -eq 0 ]]; then
        show_main_help
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Parse arguments
    local category="$1"
    local action="${2:-}"
    
    # Handle special commands
    case "$category" in
        "help"|"-h"|"--help")
            if [[ -n "$action" ]]; then
                show_category_help "$action"
            else
                show_main_help
            fi
            return 0
            ;;
        "version"|"-v"|"--version")
            show_version
            return 0
            ;;
        "search")
            if [[ -z "$action" ]]; then
                print_error "Search term required"
                print_info "Usage: mac search <term>"
                return 1
            fi
            search_commands "$action"
            return 0
            ;;
        "debug")
            enable_debug
            print_success "Debug mode enabled"
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
        "--debug")
            enable_debug
            shift
            main "$@"
            return $?
            ;;
    esac
    
    # Route to appropriate handler
    route_command "$@"
}

# =============================================================================
# Script Execution
# =============================================================================

# Only run main if script is executed directly (not sourced)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
    main "$@"
fi
